mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate on equities and lifts 20-day S&P 500 returns when inflation is not accelerating, because easier policy raises the present value of expected cash flows and supports risk appetite.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls by at least 0.25 pp from the prior monthly reading
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances do not show positive average 20d returns, or if positive returns are absent when FEDFUNDS falls while CPIAUCSL inflation is flat to lower.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above recent months signals weakening household income and demand, which depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment rises under the stated trigger but 20d SPX returns are positive in a majority of cases or the average return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates expanding payroll income and retail demand, which supports near-term equity upside through stronger revenue expectations and breadth in cyclical sectors.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in PAYEMS and RSAFS fail to improve 5d SPX returns relative to their unconditional mean, or if either series alone explains the signal as well as the pair.

mechanism_narrative: An upturn in INDPRO after a prior soft patch signals industrial acceleration and inventory restocking, which tends to support S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through improved earnings momentum.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive monthly readings
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive monthly gains in INDPRO do not precede above-zero 60d SPX returns, or if the sign of the effect is negative in a majority of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls sharply while GDPC1 growth is already slowing, the combination signals a demand rollover that weakens earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by at least 8% from its 3-month average and GDPC1 growth is below its prior-quarter pace
failure_condition: Reject if the joint housing-growth slowdown condition does not precede negative 20d SPX returns more often than not, or if returns remain positive on average after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: High FEDFUNDS restrains risk assets by raising funding costs and tightening financial conditions, so when policy is above a restrictive threshold the next 5 trading days in SPX should skew negative.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show below-zero 5d SPX returns on average, or if hit rate is not materially worse than 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A stable or falling UNRATE alongside rising PAYEMS confirms labor-market resilience, which supports consumer spending and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE does not rise and PAYEMS increases over the last 2 monthly releases
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition fails to outperform unconditional 60d SPX returns, or if rising PAYEMS without stable UNRATE yields the same effect.

mechanism_narrative: A broad demand beat measured by rising RSAFS together with stable CPIAUCSL indicates real activity without an inflation shock, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises while CPIAUCSL inflation over the same month stays below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if demand strength accompanied by non-accelerating CPIAUCSL does not lead to positive 20d SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL is excluded.
